title: Biomedical Engineer

rationales.taskAutomation: AI can accelerate device simulation and draft regulatory submission sections, but device safety validation and FDA-facing risk analysis remain firmly human.
rationales.toolMaturity: AI-assisted simulation and regulatory-drafting tools exist and are improving, but device-specific validation tooling is still maturing.
rationales.adoption: Adoption concentrates at large medical-device manufacturers; smaller device shops still rely on manual validation workflows.
rationales.agenticExposure: Agents can pre-populate 510(k) sections and run simulation batches, but device risk classification and clinical validation stay with engineers.

tasksAtRisk: Device simulation and modeling parameter sweeps
Draft sections of regulatory submissions (510(k), technical files)
tasksGrowing: Device safety risk classification and clinical validation
Novel implant and diagnostic device design
salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 17-2031, p25-p75 rounded
insight: AI drafts routine submission sections and runs device simulations, but clinical validation and regulatory risk sign-off still require an engineer to own patient safety.
